description_long: This is a large, double-headed battle axe with a broad, symmetrical blade on each side, both made of dark, weathered metal with visible scratches and wear. The axe features a central metal spike at the top, adding a piercing function to its design. The handle is constructed from wood, wrapped in textured leather or cord for improved grip, and reinforced with metal bands and decorative studs. The transition between the blade and handle is marked by ornate metal fittings, giving the axe a medieval or fantasy appearance. The overall build suggests it is both functional and decorative, suitable for display or use in themed urban environments such as parks, museums, or as a prop. The materials and craftsmanship indicate durability and a focus on both utility and visual impact.
description: A large, double-bladed battle axe with a dark metal head, central spike, and a wood handle wrapped in textured grip. Ornate metal fittings and visible wear give it a medieval, robust appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full double-bladed head, central spike, and detailed handle wrapping.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the blades and the central spike, with minimal blade width visible.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the double-bladed head and handle details from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin blade profile and central spike, similar to view 1.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical wooden handle, wrapped with textured leather or cord for grip, reinforced with metal bands, no visible curvature.
attribute_blade_design: Double-headed symmetrical blade with wide, crescent-shaped edges and a central top spike; no beard or poll, with visible wear and decorative metal fittings.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged and banded mounting with visible metal fittings securing the blade to the handle.
